skill.name: dbos-typescript
skill.description: DBOS TypeScript SDK guidance for building reliable applications with durable workflows, steps, queues, clients, and recovery-safe patterns.
skill.summary: Build reliable TypeScript applications with DBOS durable workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer produced many high-severity alerts, but review found they are documentation false positives or normal DBOS examples. Markdown code fences, impact labels, example URLs, and database configuration variables do not show malicious intent, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or executable command behavior.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are caused by Markdown backticks and TypeScript documentation examples. The reviewed lines describe DBOS rules and snippets; they do not execute shell commands from the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is inside Markdown code blocks and prose. No runtime script, shell invocation, or user-controlled command construction was found in those locations.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptography Labels
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match words in metadata and priority labels, such as impact descriptions and section names. These lines do not define hashes, ciphers, or cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows documentation metadata and headings only. There is no implementation using MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar weak primitives.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Environment Variable Examples
low_findings.description: The environment variable alerts point to standard DBOS database configuration examples. They read a database URL from the environment but do not print, transmit, or harvest secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The access pattern is ordinary application configuration for DBOS. The examples do not combine the value with network exfiltration or logging of credentials.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Network Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL and fetch alerts are documentation examples for calling external APIs from DBOS steps. The URLs are examples or references, not hidden endpoints for exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are explicitly presented as educational examples. No suspicious domain, encoded payload, credential transfer, or covert behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Durable workflow systems are hard to design correctly in TypeScript. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused DBOS guidance for workflows, steps, queues, clients, and recovery patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to configure and launch DBOS TypeScript applications.
Guides workflow and step structure for deterministic durable execution.
Covers queue concurrency, rate limits, priorities, partitioning, and deduplication.
Describes DBOSClient usage for external applications and workflow control.
Documents communication patterns with events, messages, and streams.
Provides testing, versioning, patching, and scheduling guidance.
limitations: It is documentation guidance, not the DBOS runtime package.
It does not create infrastructure or provision databases by itself.
Examples use placeholder services and must be adapted to real systems.
It does not replace current DBOS release notes or API documentation.

use_cases.title: Add durable workflows to a service
use_cases.description: Plan workflow, step, and launch patterns for a TypeScript backend that must recover after failures.

use_cases.title: Control background processing throughput
use_cases.description: Design DBOS queues with concurrency limits, rate limits, priorities, and partitioning for reliable worker pools.

use_cases.title: Review DBOS workflow correctness
use_cases.description: Check whether code keeps workflows deterministic and moves external operations into checkpointed steps.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new DBOS application.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the dbos-typescript skill to design a small DBOS TypeScript workflow. Include setup, launch order, one workflow, and one step.

prompt_templates.title: Fix workflow determinism
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of existing workflow code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the dbos-typescript skill to review this workflow for non-deterministic operations. Explain what should move into DBOS steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design queue processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the dbos-typescript skill to design DBOS queues for this workload. Include concurrency, rate limiting, priority, and worker listening rules.

prompt_templates.title: Plan safe workflow upgrades
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the dbos-typescript skill to plan a safe DBOS workflow upgrade. Cover versioning, patching, old workflow draining, and rollback concerns.

output_examples.input: How should I call an external API from a DBOS workflow?
output_examples.output: The answer explains that external calls belong in a DBOS step, because steps are checkpointed and workflows must stay deterministic.

output_examples.input: I need separate CPU and GPU workers for queued jobs.
output_examples.output: The answer recommends separate workflow queues and worker listening configuration so each worker pool only processes suitable tasks.

output_examples.input: How can I deploy a breaking workflow change safely?
output_examples.output: The answer outlines DBOS versioning and patching so old in-progress workflows can finish while new workflows use updated logic.

best_practices: Register workflows and steps before launching DBOS.
Keep workflow logic deterministic and put side effects in steps.
Use queues and workflow IDs intentionally for concurrency and idempotency.
anti_patterns: Calling external services directly inside workflow orchestration logic.
Starting workflows from inside steps or uncontrolled concurrent code.
Changing workflow step order without versioning or patching active workflows.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build TypeScript applications that use DBOS durable workflows, steps, queues, clients, and recovery patterns.

faq.question: Does this skill install DBOS?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Your project still needs the DBOS SDK and required database setup.

faq.question: Why do workflows need deterministic code?
faq.answer: DBOS may replay workflows during recovery. Deterministic workflow logic prevents replay from taking a different path.

faq.question: When should I use a DBOS step?
faq.answer: Use a step for external APIs, database calls, file operations, random values, current time, and other side effects.

faq.question: Can it help with queue design?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers DBOS queue concurrency, rate limiting, deduplication, priority, partitioning, and worker listening patterns.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed static findings were documentation false positives or normal DBOS examples, with no confirmed malicious behavior.
